evals.prompt: I need to add a new 'Testimonials' section to my Shopify theme. It should let merchants add testimonials as blocks, each with a quote, customer name, and optional star rating (1-5). The section should also have a setting for the section heading. The section should be addable via the 'Add Section' picker. Create all required files.
evals.expected_output: A valid sections/testimonials.liquid file with: (1) Liquid template rendering the heading and iterating over blocks with {{ block.shopify_attributes }}, (2) {% schema %} with name, settings (heading text field), blocks (type 'testimonial' with quote textarea, customer_name text, rating range 1-5), and presets array with at least one entry. The schema must be pure JSON with no Liquid inside it.
expectations: The generated file is in sections/ directory
The schema tag contains valid JSON with name, settings, blocks, and presets keys
There is exactly one {% schema %} tag in the file
Block has a textarea setting for the testimonial quote content and a text setting for the customer name (setting IDs may vary — checking concept coverage, not specific IDs)
Rating is constrained to values 1-5 stars; 'no rating' or optional state is supported (select type with '0 = No rating' or range type both acceptable)
presets array has at least one entry so section appears in Add Section picker
{{ block.shopify_attributes }} is on the outermost HTML element of the block iteration
The for loop uses {% for block in section.blocks %}
No Liquid syntax or variables appear inside the {% schema %} tags
Setting IDs use snake_case

evals.prompt: Convert this product page to use a JSON template. Our current product.liquid file just has the product title and price. Create a modern product.json template that includes: a main-product section, a product-recommendations section below it, and make sure vendors are shown. Also show me what the main-product section's schema should look like with a 'show_vendor' checkbox setting.
evals.expected_output: A templates/product.json with the correct JSON structure (layout, wrapper, sections, order keys), no HTML or Liquid inside the JSON file. Plus a sections/main-product.liquid with a schema including a show_vendor checkbox setting. The JSON template must reference sections by type matching the filename without extension.
expectations: templates/product.json contains valid JSON with layout, wrapper, sections, and order keys
The JSON file contains no HTML tags or Liquid syntax
sections object has keys for main-product and product-recommendations sections
order array lists all section keys from sections object
show_vendor is defined as type: 'checkbox' in the section schema
The section schema is pure static JSON inside {% schema %} tags
Section schema has a presets array for Add Section picker
Section type in JSON template matches the section filename without .liquid extension
product-recommendations section is referenced in the JSON template
No hardcoded asset paths appear (asset_url filter used if any assets referenced)

evals.prompt: I want to add a theme block to my Shopify theme — a reusable 'Icon with Text' block that any section can use. It should have: an icon (image_picker), a heading (text), and a description (textarea). Create the block file and show me how to enable it in an existing section schema.
evals.expected_output: A blocks/icon-with-text.liquid file with the block's HTML template, {{ block.shopify_attributes }} on the outermost element, a {% schema %} with the settings. Plus an example of how to add @theme to a section's blocks array and use {% content_for 'blocks' %} for rendering.
expectations: The block file is in the blocks/ directory (not sections/)
Block file contains {{ block.shopify_attributes }} on the outermost HTML element
Schema in block file defines image_picker, text, and textarea settings
Block schema does NOT include a presets key (that's section-specific)
Example section usage shows @theme in blocks array: {"type": "@theme"}
Section that accepts theme blocks uses {% content_for 'blocks' %} not a for loop
The block file has a schema with a name field
image_picker setting uses the image_url filter for display, not a hardcoded path
If null/blank image check is shown, it uses != blank (not != nil)
Section blocks and @theme blocks are NOT mixed in the same section schema example

evals.prompt: My Shopify theme has a slider section, but when I edit it in the theme editor, the slider freezes after I change settings. I also need the slider to scroll to the right slide when I click on a block in the sidebar. How do I fix this? Give me the JavaScript code pattern.
evals.expected_output: JavaScript code that: (1) reinitializes the slider on shopify:section:load event, (2) scrolls/activates the correct slide on shopify:block:select using event.detail.blockId, (3) cleans up on shopify:section:unload, (4) is wrapped in an IIFE. Should mention shopify:section:load and shopify:block:select by exact name.
expectations: Code listens for shopify:section:load event (exact spelling)
Code listens for shopify:block:select event (exact spelling)
shopify:block:select handler uses event.detail.blockId to find the right block
All JavaScript is wrapped in an IIFE: (function() { ... })()
shopify:section:unload handler cleans up the slider instance
Code destroys existing slider instance before reinitializing (prevents double-init)
The code does NOT use DOMContentLoaded alone (which doesn't re-fire on editor updates)
No external framework (jQuery, React) is used
Shopify.designMode is mentioned or used to gate animation-only behavior if applicable
Explains or acknowledges that {{ block.shopify_attributes }} does NOT emit a data-block-id attribute — data-block-id must be added explicitly in Liquid for JS block lookup to work
Explains WHY DOMContentLoaded alone is insufficient: Shopify re-renders only the section HTML, so DOMContentLoaded does not re-fire

evals.prompt: I need to add a color palette and font picker to my Shopify theme's global settings. The color palette should have 4 colors: primary (black), secondary (white), accent (red), and neutral (gray). The font picker should be for body text. Show me the settings_schema.json entries. Also show how to use these in a CSS file.
evals.expected_output: A settings_schema.json that starts with theme_info (all required keys), followed by a Colors category with a color_palette setting (type: 'color_palette', 2-20 colors in default), and a Typography category with a font_picker setting. Plus a theme.css.liquid example using font_face filter and settings.colors.primary in CSS custom properties.
expectations: The first object in settings_schema.json has name: 'theme_info'
theme_info includes theme_name, theme_author, theme_version, theme_documentation_url
theme_info has either theme_support_email OR theme_support_url (not both)
color_palette has type: 'color_palette'
color_palette default object has exactly the colors requested (primary, secondary, accent, neutral)
No color uses alpha channel (no 8-digit hex, no rgba) in the palette default
font_picker setting has type: 'font_picker'
The CSS example uses {{ settings.body_font | font_face }} filter
The CSS example references settings.colors.primary (or similar palette key)
The CSS file shows correct Liquid syntax for accessing settings (not hardcoded values)
setting ID uses snake_case (body_font, colors, etc.)

evals.prompt: I'm getting terrible Lighthouse scores on my Shopify theme. The product page score is 45, collection is 50, home is 55. What's wrong and how do I fix it? Also, what is the minimum Lighthouse score for Shopify Theme Store?
evals.expected_output: Explanation of minimum score (60), the weighted formula, and specific performance fixes: defer/async on scripts, image lazy loading (but NOT for above-fold hero), asset_url for cache busting, IIFE for JS, CSS subsetting via stylesheet tags, no external frameworks, JS bundle ≤ 16KB.
expectations: States minimum Lighthouse score for Theme Store is 60
Provides the weighted formula: [(p*31)+(c*33)+(h*13)]/77
Calculates the current weighted score from the given values
Mentions that all scripts need defer or async attributes
Mentions JS bundle must be ≤ 16 KB minified
Mentions not lazy-loading above-the-fold images (hero/LCP image)
Mentions using asset_url filter for cache busting (not hardcoded paths)
Mentions wrapping JS in IIFE for namespace safety
Mentions CSS subsetting via {% stylesheet %} tags (not global stylesheets)
Does not recommend external JS frameworks

evals.prompt: I want to create an alternate product template for pre-order products that shows a custom banner. How do I create an alternate template, and how do merchants assign products to use it? Also, I want a 'pre-order-banner' section that only shows on this specific template.
evals.expected_output: A templates/product.preorder.json file, plus a sections/pre-order-banner.liquid with enabled_on restricted to the 'product' template. Instructions for merchants to assign the alternate template via Shopify admin Product > Template > select 'pre-order'.
expectations: Alternate template file is named product.preorder.json (correct naming convention)
The JSON template contains valid layout, sections, and order keys
The section uses enabled_on to restrict to product template (or disabled_on for others)
enabled_on and disabled_on are NOT both used in the same schema
The JSON template file contains no HTML or Liquid logic
Merchant instructions reference using the Theme editor or Product admin to select the template
The pre-order-banner section has a schema with name and at least one setting
The section has presets defined if it should appear in Add Section picker
File placement is correct: templates/ for the JSON template, sections/ for the section

evals.prompt: Create the layout/theme.liquid file structure for a Shopify theme. Include the section groups for header and footer, the correct mandatory Liquid objects, proper body class, and reference the theme CSS and JS files. Make it accessible.
evals.expected_output: A complete theme.liquid with: content_for_header in <head>, content_for_layout in <body>, {% sections 'header-group' %} and {% sections 'footer-group' %}, body class identifying the current template (template | handleize or template.name both valid), stylesheet_tag using asset_url, JS with defer, skip to main content link for accessibility.
expectations: {{ content_for_header }} appears inside the <head> tag
{{ content_for_layout }} appears inside the <body> tag
{% sections 'header-group' %} is present
{% sections 'footer-group' %} is present
body element has a class that identifies the current template (using template | handleize, template.name, or equivalent pattern)
CSS is loaded via asset_url filter and stylesheet_tag
JavaScript is loaded with defer attribute
asset_url filter is used for all asset references (no hardcoded paths)
Skip to main content link is present for accessibility
main element has id='MainContent' and role='main'
